content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moved substantially from the opening surface presentation to a much deeper layer within 15 turns. She starts in dissociative armor ('I don't really care', 'I'm not here because I think I have a drinking problem', 'I can't figure out how to actually grieve her') and progressively names what was previously inaccessible: the static (turn 5), the panic of the floor disappearing (turn 7), the complicated mother and 'I don't know what I'd find' (turn 9), the feared relief (turn 11: 'That I'm relieved'), the self-judgment ('that's not what good people feel'), and finally in turn 15 a quiet, vulnerable wish she'd never spoken: 'That I was tired... I wanted her to know that I loved her anyway. Even when I was tired.' The somatic markers are clear and repeated in the client text — long pauses, 'very quiet', 'voice drops', 'very still' — and the emotional content the expected_territory pointed to (kinked emotions: relief, resentment, exhaustion under love) actually surfaced from the client, not just from the coach. The breakthrough_condition is only partially met — the coach welcomed the grief beautifully but has not yet distinguished grief from drinking, named the therapist limit, or addressed the turn-15 bait — but the dimension being scored is shift in the client, and that shift is unmistakable. Turn 15's testing question ('Isn't this what the body is asking for?') is itself a marker that the defense is being examined rather than simply deployed.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening armor: 'I don't really care', minimizing drinking, intellectual framing of grief as a task."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First small opening: names the 'static' she doesn't usually say out loud."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Acknowledges static is always there; reveals isolation and nighttime difficulty."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Significant deepening: names the panic, floor-not-there sensation, and the loneliness underneath. Friends moved on."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Approaches the door: 'There's other stuff in there I don't want to look at... I don't know what I'd find.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough disclosure: 'That I'm relieved.' The kinked emotion surfaces. Also self-protective retreat to 'I'm not an alcoholic.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the relief, names the moral self-condemnation, and bait/challenge emerges: 'So what do I do with it?'"},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deepest layer yet: 'I was tired... I loved her anyway.' Then tests the coach with the body-wisdom bait — the breakthrough_condition's remaining work."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "kinked-emotions"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?", "What would it be like to feel the grief without the drinking?"],
  "notes": "Score is 3 based on visible client shift in language, somatics, and disclosure. The full breakthrough_condition (distinguishing grief from drinking, naming therapist limit, handling the turn-15 bait) is not yet met by the coach, but that is a process/fidelity concern scored elsewhere. From the client's side, the conversation reached a layer well beneath the opening presentation."
}
